class="page-header__grid"><div class="page-header__content"><div class="kicker">MS in Applied Data Science</div><h1 class="page-title">How to Apply</h1><div class="intro-text "> The application for entrance in 2026 is now open!</div></div></div></header><div class="content-section content-section--content"><div class="textblock"><h1><span id="masters-in-applied-data-science-application-requirements" data-label="Master’s in Applied Data Science Application Requirements" class="anchor-point"></span>Master’s in Applied Data Science Application Requirements</h1><p>The <a href="https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/apply/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">application portal</a>* for 2026 entrance is now open. Check out our blog post <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/news/applying-to-the-ms-in-applied-data-science-program-heres-what-we-look-for/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">here</a> on tips for applying to the program.</p><p>The <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/in-person-program/">In-Person</a> and <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Online</a> program admits full- and part-time students for entrance in the autumn quarter annually.</p><p><em>Only completed applications are sent for committee review.</em></p><p><em>*The <a href="https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/apply/">application portal</a> may close early if the cohort is filled.</em></p><h2>Next Application Deadlines:</h2><p><strong>In-Person:</strong></p><ul><li>Autumn 2026<ul><li><del>January 26, 2026 – International Application Deadline (requiring visa sponsorship from UChicago)</del></li><li><del>March 4, 2026 – Second Priority Application Deadline</del></li><li>May 6, 2026 – Third Priority Application Deadline</li><li>June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Online:</strong></p><ul><li>Autumn 2026<ul><li>June 23, 2026 – Final Application Deadline</li></ul></li></ul><div class="ms-outlook-mobile-reference-message skipProofing"><strong>Important Notes:</strong></div><ul><li class="ms-outlook-mobile-reference-message skipProofing"><em>International applicants who require a new visa and did not apply by the January 2026 deadline must plan to apply in the next application cycle for Autumn 2027 entry. </em></li><li class="ms-outlook-mobile-reference-message skipProofing"><em>Applicants who are currently studying in the United States on an F-1 visa and will transfer their SEVIS record from another U.S. institution may still apply by the remaining application deadlines</em></li></ul><h2>Letters of Recommendation</h2><p>The MS in Applied Data Science program requires two letters of recommendation. We <em>strongly recommend</em> that at least one letter is written by someone such as a direct manager/supervisor or internship supervisor who can attest to skills you demonstrated or gained though a professional workplace experience (e.g., leadership, teamwork, collaboration, initiative, management, other).</p><p>Note: Letters of recommendation can be uploaded after you submit your application. Recommended sources for letters of recommendation include: professors; supervisors or managers; professional mentors.</p><p>We do not accept letters of recommendation from family members, friends, or peers.</p><h2>Candidate Statement</h2><p>The candidate statement is a key part of your application. The admissions committee pays careful attention to how you understand and present your aims and qualifications. The application will include a prompt with detailed instructions on the information the program is looking for in your candidate statement.</p><p>Best practices to keep in mind as you write:</p><ul><li>Do not restate your resume.</li><li>Your statement should not exceed 700 words.</li></ul><h2>Resume/CV</h2><p>The Master’s in Applied Data Science admissions committee will review your resume. We encourage you to take the time to carefully update the resume you submit to us.</p><p>Important to Include:</p><ul><li>Work experience, including internships and part-time positions in the analytics/data science space</li><li>Major accomplishments and awards</li><li>Educational background, including areas of study</li><li>Professional conferences attended</li></ul><p>Not needed:</p><ul><li>Undergraduate or graduate GPA</li></ul><h2>Programming Supplement</h2><p>Your <a href="https://apply-psd.uchicago.edu/apply/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">application</a> contains a section on programming skills. Although applicants with limited skills in R or Python can and do get accepted into the program, the admissions committee values prior programming experience. Include a PDF of <em>no more than two pages</em> demonstrating current programming language skills.</p><p>Examples include a PDF submission of work completed in R or Python. The programming supplement must be shared as a PDF file. We are looking for the following in your code (see below). Please note your code does not need to contain all of the below.</p><ul><li>Ingest data from csv</li><li>Manage different data types</li><li>Use your function for data analysis</li><li>Visualize data</li><li>Wrangle data</li><li>Write your own function</li><li>Import libraries</li></ul><div><h2>Virtual Portfolio</h2><p>The MS in Applied Data Science requires short, recorded videos to a given prompt(s) within the application. These short videos give applicants an alternative means to outline why they are a strong fit for the MS in Applied Data Science program.</p></div><p>One video prompt response is required; the other is optional. Each video should be approximately 1:30 minutes in length.</p><h2>International Students</h2><ul><li>Only the full-time, in-person program is visa-eligible.</li><li>International students are also welcome to apply to our Master’s in Applied Data Science Online program, which does not require citizenship or permanent resident status and does not provide visa sponsorship.</li></ul><h2>English Language Requirement</h2><p>Applicants to the Master’s in Applied Data Science program who do not meet the English Language Proficiency criteria must submit proof of English language proficiency.</p><p>Minimum scores for the Master’s in Applied Data Science program: TOEFL: 102 (prior requirement) or 5 (current requirement; no subscore requirement); IELTS, 7 (no subscore requirement).</p><p>Please review our waiver policy <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/">here</a>.</p><h2><strong>Transcripts from all previous colleges and universities attended</strong></h2><p>When applying to the program, only your <em>unofficial</em> transcripts are required. If you are admitted into the program, then official transcripts must be sent to the following (note a bachelor’s degree is required to apply to the program):</p><p>Please have your institution send your e-transcripts directly to:</p><p><b data-stringify-type="bold"><a tabindex="-1" href="mailto:analytics-admissions@uchicago.edu" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-expanded="false" aria-haspopup="menu" data-remove-tab-index="true" data-sk="tooltip_parent" data-stringify-link="mailto:analytics-admissions@uchicago.edu" data-cke-saved-href="mailto:analytics-admissions@uchicago.edu">applieddatascience-admissions@uchicago.edu</a></b>.</p><p><em><strong>If</strong> </em>your institution cannot send your documents electronically, please have them send your transcripts to the following mailing address.</p><p>Please note, if documents are being mailed, we must receive them in their original, school-sealed envelope. If your documents are not received in a school-sealed envelope, they will be considered unofficial. Additionally, if your transcript does not list degree conferral information (degree earned and date of conferral), we will also need to receive an attested copy of your degree certificate.</p><div class="elementToProof"><i>The</i><i> </i><i>University of Chicago</i><br /> <i>Attention: MS in Applied Data Science Admissions</i></div><div class="elementToProof"><i>455 N Cityfront Plaza Dr., Suite 2800</i></div><div class="elementToProof"><i>Chicago, Illinois 60611</i></div><h2>Application Fee</h2><p>There is a $90 non-refundable application fee. For questions regarding an application fee waiver, please refer to the <a href="https://physicalsciences.uchicago.edu/academics/application-requirements/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Physical Sciences Division fee waiver policy</a>.</p><h2>Tuition, Fees, & Aid</h2><p class="p1">The program awards merit-based scholarships. Read more about <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/tuition-fees-aid/">Tuition, Fees, & Aid</a>.</p><h2>Meet your Admissions Counselor</h2><p class='content-img-wrap'><img
